# SearchReplace.Tests.ps1
# TDD tests for multi-file search and replace functionality
# Uses Pester as the test framework

Set-StrictMode -Latest
$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'

# Import the module under test
$modulePath = Join-Path $PSScriptRoot 'SearchReplace.ps1'
. $modulePath

# ============================================================
# Test fixtures - create mock directory structures for testing
# ============================================================

function New-TestFixtures {
    [CmdletBinding()]
    [OutputType([string])]
    param(
        [string]$BasePath
    )

    $testDir = Join-Path $BasePath 'test-fixtures'

    # Create directory structure
    $null = New-Item -Path $testDir -ItemType Directory -Force
    $null = New-Item -Path (Join-Path $testDir 'subdir1') -ItemType Directory -Force
    $null = New-Item -Path (Join-Path $testDir 'subdir2') -ItemType Directory -Force
    $null = New-Item -Path (Join-Path $testDir 'subdir2' 'nested') -ItemType Directory -Force

    # Create test files with known content
    Set-Content -Path (Join-Path $testDir 'file1.txt') -Value @"
Hello World
This is a test file
foo bar baz
The quick brown fox
"@

    Set-Content -Path (Join-Path $testDir 'file2.txt') -Value @"
Another file
foo appears here too
Nothing special
"@

    Set-Content -Path (Join-Path $testDir 'subdir1' 'file3.txt') -Value @"
Subdirectory file
foo and bar
"@

    Set-Content -Path (Join-Path $testDir 'subdir2' 'file4.log') -Value @"
Log entry: foo
Log entry: bar
"@

    Set-Content -Path (Join-Path $testDir 'subdir2' 'nested' 'file5.txt') -Value @"
Deeply nested
foo is here
"@

    # Create a file with no matches for negative testing
    Set-Content -Path (Join-Path $testDir 'nomatch.txt') -Value @"
This file has no matches
for our pattern
"@

    return $testDir
}

function Remove-TestFixtures {
    [CmdletBinding()]
    [OutputType([void])]
    param(
        [string]$BasePath
    )
    if (Test-Path $BasePath) {
        Remove-Item -Path $BasePath -Recurse -Force
    }
}

# ============================================================
# Describe block: Get-FilesMatchingGlob
# TDD Cycle 1: Find files matching a glob pattern recursively
# ============================================================

Describe 'Get-FilesMatchingGlob' {
    BeforeAll {
        $script:tempDir = Join-Path ([System.IO.Path]::GetTempPath()) "SearchReplaceTests_$(New-Guid)"
        $script:fixtureDir = New-TestFixtures -BasePath $script:tempDir
    }

    AfterAll {
        Remove-TestFixtures -BasePath $script:tempDir
    }

    It 'should find files matching *.txt pattern recursively' {
        $files = Get-FilesMatchingGlob -RootPath $script:fixtureDir -GlobPattern '*.txt'
        $fileNames = $files | ForEach-Object { [System.IO.Path]::GetFileName($_) }

        $fileNames | Should -Contain 'file1.txt'
        $fileNames | Should -Contain 'file2.txt'
        $fileNames | Should -Contain 'nomatch.txt'
        $fileNames | Should -Contain 'file3.txt'
        $fileNames | Should -Contain 'file5.txt'
    }

    It 'should not return files that do not match the pattern' {
        $files = Get-FilesMatchingGlob -RootPath $script:fixtureDir -GlobPattern '*.txt'
        $fileNames = $files | ForEach-Object { [System.IO.Path]::GetFileName($_) }

        $fileNames | Should -Not -Contain 'file4.log'
    }

    It 'should find files matching *.log pattern' {
        $files = Get-FilesMatchingGlob -RootPath $script:fixtureDir -GlobPattern '*.log'
        $fileNames = $files | ForEach-Object { [System.IO.Path]::GetFileName($_) }

        $fileNames | Should -Contain 'file4.log'
        $fileNames.Count | Should -Be 1
    }

    It 'should find files matching wildcard for specific names' {
        $files = Get-FilesMatchingGlob -RootPath $script:fixtureDir -GlobPattern 'file*.txt'
        $fileNames = $files | ForEach-Object { [System.IO.Path]::GetFileName($_) }

        $fileNames | Should -Contain 'file1.txt'
        $fileNames | Should -Contain 'file2.txt'
        $fileNames | Should -Not -Contain 'nomatch.txt'
    }

    It 'should return empty array when no files match' {
        $files = Get-FilesMatchingGlob -RootPath $script:fixtureDir -GlobPattern '*.xml'
        $files | Should -BeNullOrEmpty
    }

    It 'should throw when root path does not exist' {
        { Get-FilesMatchingGlob -RootPath 'C:\NonExistent\Path' -GlobPattern '*.txt' } | Should -Throw
    }
}

# ============================================================
# Describe block: Search-FileForPattern
# TDD Cycle 2: Search a single file for regex pattern matches
# ============================================================

Describe 'Search-FileForPattern' {
    BeforeAll {
        $script:tempDir = Join-Path ([System.IO.Path]::GetTempPath()) "SearchReplaceTests_$(New-Guid)"
        $script:fixtureDir = New-TestFixtures -BasePath $script:tempDir
    }

    AfterAll {
        Remove-TestFixtures -BasePath $script:tempDir
    }

    It 'should return matches with correct line numbers' {
        $filePath = Join-Path $script:fixtureDir 'file1.txt'
        $matches = Search-FileForPattern -FilePath $filePath -SearchPattern 'foo'

        # file1.txt line 3: "foo bar baz"
        $matches | Should -Not -BeNullOrEmpty
        $lineNumbers = $matches | ForEach-Object { $_.LineNumber }
        $lineNumbers | Should -Contain 3
    }

    It 'should return the matching line text' {
        $filePath = Join-Path $script:fixtureDir 'file1.txt'
        $matches = Search-FileForPattern -FilePath $filePath -SearchPattern 'foo'

        $matchLines = $matches | ForEach-Object { $_.LineText }
        $matchLines | Should -Contain 'foo bar baz'
    }

    It 'should return empty array when no matches found' {
        $filePath = Join-Path $script:fixtureDir 'nomatch.txt'
        $matches = Search-FileForPattern -FilePath $filePath -SearchPattern 'foo'

        $matches | Should -BeNullOrEmpty
    }

    It 'should support regex patterns' {
        $filePath = Join-Path $script:fixtureDir 'file1.txt'
        $matches = Search-FileForPattern -FilePath $filePath -SearchPattern 'fo+'

        $matches | Should -Not -BeNullOrEmpty
        $matches[0].LineText | Should -Match 'foo'
    }

    It 'should find multiple matches in the same file' {
        $filePath = Join-Path $script:fixtureDir 'file2.txt'
        # file2.txt has "foo" on line 2
        $matches = Search-FileForPattern -FilePath $filePath -SearchPattern 'foo'

        $matches.Count | Should -BeGreaterOrEqual 1
    }

    It 'should include file path in each match result' {
        $filePath = Join-Path $script:fixtureDir 'file1.txt'
        $matches = Search-FileForPattern -FilePath $filePath -SearchPattern 'foo'

        $matches[0].FilePath | Should -Be $filePath
    }

    It 'should throw when file does not exist' {
        { Search-FileForPattern -FilePath 'C:\NonExistent\file.txt' -SearchPattern 'foo' } | Should -Throw
    }
}

# ============================================================
# Describe block: Invoke-FileReplace
# TDD Cycle 3: Perform replacement in a file with backup creation
# ============================================================

Describe 'Invoke-FileReplace' {
    BeforeEach {
        $script:tempDir = Join-Path ([System.IO.Path]::GetTempPath()) "SearchReplaceTests_$(New-Guid)"
        $script:fixtureDir = New-TestFixtures -BasePath $script:tempDir
    }

    AfterEach {
        Remove-TestFixtures -BasePath $script:tempDir
    }

    It 'should replace text in file and return change records' {
        $filePath = Join-Path $script:fixtureDir 'file1.txt'
        $changes = Invoke-FileReplace -FilePath $filePath -SearchPattern 'foo' -Replacement 'BAR' -CreateBackup $false

        $content = Get-Content -Path $filePath -Raw
        $content | Should -Match 'BAR'
        $content | Should -Not -Match '\bfoo\b'
        $changes | Should -Not -BeNullOrEmpty
    }

    It 'should return change records with old and new text' {
        $filePath = Join-Path $script:fixtureDir 'file1.txt'
        $changes = Invoke-FileReplace -FilePath $filePath -SearchPattern 'foo' -Replacement 'BAR' -CreateBackup $false

        $change = $changes | Where-Object { $_.OldText -match 'foo' } | Select-Object -First 1
        $change | Should -Not -BeNullOrEmpty
        $change.OldText | Should -Match 'foo'
        $change.NewText | Should -Match 'BAR'
    }

    It 'should create backup file when CreateBackup is true' {
        $filePath = Join-Path $script:fixtureDir 'file1.txt'
        $null = Invoke-FileReplace -FilePath $filePath -SearchPattern 'foo' -Replacement 'BAR' -CreateBackup $true

        $backupPath = "$filePath.bak"
        Test-Path -Path $backupPath | Should -BeTrue
    }

    It 'should backup file contains original content' {
        $filePath = Join-Path $script:fixtureDir 'file1.txt'
        $originalContent = Get-Content -Path $filePath -Raw

        $null = Invoke-FileReplace -FilePath $filePath -SearchPattern 'foo' -Replacement 'BAR' -CreateBackup $true

        $backupContent = Get-Content -Path "$filePath.bak" -Raw
        $backupContent | Should -Be $originalContent
    }

    It 'should not create backup file when CreateBackup is false' {
        $filePath = Join-Path $script:fixtureDir 'file1.txt'
        $null = Invoke-FileReplace -FilePath $filePath -SearchPattern 'foo' -Replacement 'BAR' -CreateBackup $false

        $backupPath = "$filePath.bak"
        Test-Path -Path $backupPath | Should -BeFalse
    }

    It 'should return empty array when no matches found' {
        $filePath = Join-Path $script:fixtureDir 'nomatch.txt'
        $changes = Invoke-FileReplace -FilePath $filePath -SearchPattern 'foo' -Replacement 'BAR' -CreateBackup $false

        $changes | Should -BeNullOrEmpty
        # File should be unchanged
        $content = Get-Content -Path $filePath -Raw
        $content | Should -Not -Match 'BAR'
    }

    It 'should include line number in change records' {
        $filePath = Join-Path $script:fixtureDir 'file1.txt'
        $changes = Invoke-FileReplace -FilePath $filePath -SearchPattern 'foo' -Replacement 'BAR' -CreateBackup $false

        $changes[0].LineNumber | Should -BeGreaterThan 0
    }

    It 'should throw when file does not exist' {
        { Invoke-FileReplace -FilePath 'C:\NonExistent\file.txt' -SearchPattern 'foo' -Replacement 'BAR' -CreateBackup $false } | Should -Throw
    }
}

# ============================================================
# Describe block: Invoke-MultiFileSearchReplace (Preview Mode)
# TDD Cycle 4: Preview mode - show matches without modifying files
# ============================================================

Describe 'Invoke-MultiFileSearchReplace - Preview Mode' {
    BeforeAll {
        $script:tempDir = Join-Path ([System.IO.Path]::GetTempPath()) "SearchReplaceTests_$(New-Guid)"
        $script:fixtureDir = New-TestFixtures -BasePath $script:tempDir
    }

    AfterAll {
        Remove-TestFixtures -BasePath $script:tempDir
    }

    It 'should return match results without modifying files in preview mode' {
        $originalContent = Get-Content -Path (Join-Path $script:fixtureDir 'file1.txt') -Raw

        $results = Invoke-MultiFileSearchReplace `
            -RootPath $script:fixtureDir `
            -GlobPattern '*.txt' `
            -SearchPattern 'foo' `
            -Replacement 'BAR' `
            -Preview $true `
            -CreateBackup $false

        # File should not be modified
        $newContent = Get-Content -Path (Join-Path $script:fixtureDir 'file1.txt') -Raw
        $newContent | Should -Be $originalContent

        # Results should contain match info
        $results | Should -Not -BeNullOrEmpty
    }

    It 'should return results with FilePath, LineNumber, OldText fields in preview mode' {
        $results = Invoke-MultiFileSearchReplace `
            -RootPath $script:fixtureDir `
            -GlobPattern '*.txt' `
            -SearchPattern 'foo' `
            -Replacement 'BAR' `
            -Preview $true `
            -CreateBackup $false

        $result = $results | Select-Object -First 1
        $result.PSObject.Properties.Name | Should -Contain 'FilePath'
        $result.PSObject.Properties.Name | Should -Contain 'LineNumber'
        $result.PSObject.Properties.Name | Should -Contain 'OldText'
    }

    It 'should find matches across multiple files in preview mode' {
        $results = Invoke-MultiFileSearchReplace `
            -RootPath $script:fixtureDir `
            -GlobPattern '*.txt' `
            -SearchPattern 'foo' `
            -Replacement 'BAR' `
            -Preview $true `
            -CreateBackup $false

        $distinctFiles = $results | ForEach-Object { $_.FilePath } | Select-Object -Unique
        $distinctFiles.Count | Should -BeGreaterThan 1
    }

    It 'should not create backup files in preview mode' {
        $null = Invoke-MultiFileSearchReplace `
            -RootPath $script:fixtureDir `
            -GlobPattern '*.txt' `
            -SearchPattern 'foo' `
            -Replacement 'BAR' `
            -Preview $true `
            -CreateBackup $true

        # Even with CreateBackup=$true, preview mode should not create backups
        $backupFiles = Get-ChildItem -Path $script:fixtureDir -Filter '*.bak' -Recurse
        $backupFiles | Should -BeNullOrEmpty
    }
}

# ============================================================
# Describe block: Invoke-MultiFileSearchReplace (Replace Mode)
# TDD Cycle 5: Replace mode - modify files and generate report
# ============================================================

Describe 'Invoke-MultiFileSearchReplace - Replace Mode' {
    BeforeEach {
        $script:tempDir = Join-Path ([System.IO.Path]::GetTempPath()) "SearchReplaceTests_$(New-Guid)"
        $script:fixtureDir = New-TestFixtures -BasePath $script:tempDir
    }

    AfterEach {
        Remove-TestFixtures -BasePath $script:tempDir
    }

    It 'should modify files matching glob pattern' {
        $null = Invoke-MultiFileSearchReplace `
            -RootPath $script:fixtureDir `
            -GlobPattern '*.txt' `
            -SearchPattern 'foo' `
            -Replacement 'REPLACED' `
            -Preview $false `
            -CreateBackup $false

        $content = Get-Content -Path (Join-Path $script:fixtureDir 'file1.txt') -Raw
        $content | Should -Match 'REPLACED'
        $content | Should -Not -Match '\bfoo\b'
    }

    It 'should create backup files when CreateBackup is true' {
        $null = Invoke-MultiFileSearchReplace `
            -RootPath $script:fixtureDir `
            -GlobPattern '*.txt' `
            -SearchPattern 'foo' `
            -Replacement 'REPLACED' `
            -Preview $false `
            -CreateBackup $true

        $backupFiles = Get-ChildItem -Path $script:fixtureDir -Filter '*.bak' -Recurse
        $backupFiles | Should -Not -BeNullOrEmpty
    }

    It 'should return summary report with FilePath, LineNumber, OldText, NewText' {
        $report = Invoke-MultiFileSearchReplace `
            -RootPath $script:fixtureDir `
            -GlobPattern '*.txt' `
            -SearchPattern 'foo' `
            -Replacement 'REPLACED' `
            -Preview $false `
            -CreateBackup $false

        $report | Should -Not -BeNullOrEmpty
        $entry = $report | Select-Object -First 1
        $entry.PSObject.Properties.Name | Should -Contain 'FilePath'
        $entry.PSObject.Properties.Name | Should -Contain 'LineNumber'
        $entry.PSObject.Properties.Name | Should -Contain 'OldText'
        $entry.PSObject.Properties.Name | Should -Contain 'NewText'
    }

    It 'should report correct old and new text in summary' {
        $report = Invoke-MultiFileSearchReplace `
            -RootPath $script:fixtureDir `
            -GlobPattern '*.txt' `
            -SearchPattern 'foo' `
            -Replacement 'REPLACED' `
            -Preview $false `
            -CreateBackup $false

        $entry = $report | Where-Object { $_.OldText -match 'foo' } | Select-Object -First 1
        $entry | Should -Not -BeNullOrEmpty
        $entry.OldText | Should -Match 'foo'
        $entry.NewText | Should -Match 'REPLACED'
    }

    It 'should only replace in files matching glob pattern' {
        $null = Invoke-MultiFileSearchReplace `
            -RootPath $script:fixtureDir `
            -GlobPattern '*.log' `
            -SearchPattern 'foo' `
            -Replacement 'REPLACED' `
            -Preview $false `
            -CreateBackup $false

        # .txt files should be unchanged
        $txtContent = Get-Content -Path (Join-Path $script:fixtureDir 'file1.txt') -Raw
        $txtContent | Should -Match '\bfoo\b'
        $txtContent | Should -Not -Match 'REPLACED'

        # .log files should be changed
        $logContent = Get-Content -Path (Join-Path $script:fixtureDir 'subdir2' 'file4.log') -Raw
        $logContent | Should -Match 'REPLACED'
    }

    It 'should handle files with no matches gracefully' {
        $report = Invoke-MultiFileSearchReplace `
            -RootPath $script:fixtureDir `
            -GlobPattern '*.txt' `
            -SearchPattern 'ZZZNOMATCH' `
            -Replacement 'REPLACED' `
            -Preview $false `
            -CreateBackup $false

        $report | Should -BeNullOrEmpty
    }

    It 'should process files in subdirectories recursively' {
        $report = Invoke-MultiFileSearchReplace `
            -RootPath $script:fixtureDir `
            -GlobPattern '*.txt' `
            -SearchPattern 'foo' `
            -Replacement 'REPLACED' `
            -Preview $false `

Write-Host "`nDemo complete." -ForegroundColor Green

1→# SearchReplace.ps1
     2→# Multi-file search and replace with preview mode, backup creation, and summary reporting.
     3→# All functions use strict mode, typed parameters, and OutputType declarations.
     4→
     5→Set-StrictMode -Latest
     6→$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
     7→
     8→# ============================================================
     9→# Function: Get-FilesMatchingGlob
    10→# Returns all files under RootPath whose names match GlobPattern.
    11→# Uses Get-ChildItem -Recurse with -Filter for efficient glob matching.
    12→# ============================================================
    13→function Get-FilesMatchingGlob {
    14→    [CmdletBinding()]
    15→    [OutputType([string[]])]
    16→    param(
    17→        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
    18→        [string]$RootPath,
    19→
    20→        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
    21→        [string]$GlobPattern
    22→    )
    23→
    24→    if (-not (Test-Path -Path $RootPath -PathType Container)) {
    25→        throw "Root path does not exist or is not a directory: '$RootPath'"
    26→    }
    27→
    28→    # Use -Filter for glob matching; -Recurse searches all subdirectories.
    29→    # Wrap in @() to always get an array even when zero or one file matches.
    30→    [object[]]$files = @(Get-ChildItem -Path $RootPath -Filter $GlobPattern -Recurse -File -ErrorAction Stop)
    31→
    32→    # Build result using a generic list for type safety
    33→    [System.Collections.Generic.List[string]]$resultList = [System.Collections.Generic.List[string]]::new()
    34→    foreach ($file in $files) {
    35→        $resultList.Add([string]$file.FullName)
    36→    }
    37→
    38→    return [string[]]$resultList.ToArray()
    39→}
    40→
    41→# ============================================================
    42→# Function: Search-FileForPattern
    43→# Searches a single file for a regex pattern.
    44→# Returns an array of match result objects with FilePath, LineNumber, LineText.
    45→# ============================================================
    46→function Search-FileForPattern {
    47→    [CmdletBinding()]
    48→    [OutputType([PSCustomObject[]])]
    49→    param(
    50→        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
    51→        [string]$FilePath,
    52→
    53→        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
    54→        [string]$SearchPattern
    55→    )
    56→
    57→    if (-not (Test-Path -Path $FilePath -PathType Leaf)) {
    58→        throw "File does not exist: '$FilePath'"
    59→    }
    60→
    61→    [System.Collections.Generic.List[PSCustomObject]]$results = [System.Collections.Generic.List[PSCustomObject]]::new()
    62→    [int]$lineNumber = 0
    63→
    64→    # Read file line by line for accurate line number tracking.
    65→    # Use @() to ensure we always have an array (handles empty files gracefully).
    66→    [object[]]$lines = @(Get-Content -Path $FilePath -ErrorAction Stop)
    67→
    68→    foreach ($line in $lines) {
    69→        $lineNumber++
    70→        # Use -match for regex testing; result is stored in automatic $Matches variable
    71→        if ([string]$line -match $SearchPattern) {
    72→            $results.Add([PSCustomObject]@{
    73→                FilePath   = [string]$FilePath
    74→                LineNumber = [int]$lineNumber
    75→                LineText   = [string]$line
    76→            })
    77→        }
    78→    }
    79→
    80→    return [PSCustomObject[]]$results.ToArray()
    81→}
    82→
    83→# ============================================================
    84→# Function: Invoke-FileReplace
    85→# Performs search-and-replace on a single file.
    86→# Optionally creates a .bak backup before modifying.
    87→# Returns an array of change records: FilePath, LineNumber, OldText, NewText.
    88→# ============================================================
    89→function Invoke-FileReplace {
    90→    [CmdletBinding()]
    91→    [OutputType([PSCustomObject[]])]
    92→    param(
    93→        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
    94→        [string]$FilePath,
    95→
    96→        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
    97→        [string]$SearchPattern,
    98→
    99→        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
   100→        [string]$Replacement,
   101→
   102→        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
   103→        [bool]$CreateBackup
   104→    )
   105→
   106→    if (-not (Test-Path -Path $FilePath -PathType Leaf)) {
   107→        throw "File does not exist: '$FilePath'"
   108→    }
   109→
   110→    # Read all lines; @() ensures array even for single-line or empty files
   111→    [object[]]$originalLines = @(Get-Content -Path $FilePath -ErrorAction Stop)
   112→    [System.Collections.Generic.List[PSCustomObject]]$changes = [System.Collections.Generic.List[PSCustomObject]]::new()
   113→
   114→    [bool]$hasChanges = $false
   115→    [System.Collections.Generic.List[string]]$newLines = [System.Collections.Generic.List[string]]::new()
   116→
   117→    [int]$lineNumber = 0
   118→    foreach ($line in $originalLines) {
   119→        $lineNumber++
   120→        [string]$lineStr = [string]$line
   121→        if ($lineStr -match $SearchPattern) {
   122→            # Apply regex replacement; -replace uses .NET regex engine
   123→            [string]$newLine = $lineStr -replace $SearchPattern, $Replacement
   124→            $newLines.Add($newLine)
   125→            $changes.Add([PSCustomObject]@{
   126→                FilePath   = [string]$FilePath
   127→                LineNumber = [int]$lineNumber
   128→                OldText    = $lineStr
   129→                NewText    = $newLine
   130→            })
   131→            $hasChanges = $true
   132→        } else {
   133→            $newLines.Add($lineStr)
   134→        }
   135→    }
   136→
   137→    # Only write if changes were made to avoid unnecessary file modification timestamps
   138→    if ($hasChanges) {
   139→        # Create backup before modifying if requested
   140→        if ($CreateBackup) {
   141→            [string]$backupPath = [string]"$FilePath.bak"
   142→            Copy-Item -Path $FilePath -Destination $backupPath -Force -ErrorAction Stop
   143→        }
   144→
   145→        # Write modified content back to file
   146→        Set-Content -Path $FilePath -Value $newLines.ToArray() -ErrorAction Stop
   147→    }
   148→
   149→    return [PSCustomObject[]]$changes.ToArray()
   150→}
   151→
   152→# ============================================================
   153→# Function: Invoke-MultiFileSearchReplace
   154→# Main entry point: searches all files matching GlobPattern under RootPath.
   155→# In preview mode ($Preview = $true): shows matches without modifying files.
   156→# In replace mode ($Preview = $false): performs replacements and creates backups if requested.
   157→# Returns array of change/match records for use in summary report.
   158→# ============================================================
   159→function Invoke-MultiFileSearchReplace {
   160→    [CmdletBinding()]
   161→    [OutputType([PSCustomObject[]])]
   162→    param(
   163→        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
   164→        [string]$RootPath,
   165→
   166→        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
   167→        [string]$GlobPattern,
   168→
   169→        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
   170→        [string]$SearchPattern,
   171→
   172→        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
   173→        [string]$Replacement,
   174→
   175→        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
   176→        [bool]$Preview,
   177→
   178→        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
   179→        [bool]$CreateBackup
   180→    )
   181→
   182→    # Step 1: Find all files matching the glob pattern.
   183→    # @() wraps the result to guarantee a non-null array even when there are no matches.
   184→    [string[]]$matchingFiles = @(Get-FilesMatchingGlob -RootPath $RootPath -GlobPattern $GlobPattern)
   185→
   186→    if ($matchingFiles.Count -eq 0) {
   187→        Write-Verbose "No files matched pattern '$GlobPattern' under '$RootPath'"
   188→        return [PSCustomObject[]]@()
   189→    }
   190→
   191→    [System.Collections.Generic.List[PSCustomObject]]$allResults = [System.Collections.Generic.List[PSCustomObject]]::new()
   192→
   193→    if ($Preview) {
   194→        # Preview mode: search only — do NOT modify files, do NOT create backups
   195→        foreach ($file in $matchingFiles) {
   196→            # @() ensures foreach never receives $null when there are no matches
   197→            foreach ($matchItem in @(Search-FileForPattern -FilePath $file -SearchPattern $SearchPattern)) {
   198→                # Compute what the replacement would look like (for preview context)
   199→                [string]$previewNewText = [string]$matchItem.LineText -replace $SearchPattern, $Replacement
   200→                $allResults.Add([PSCustomObject]@{
   201→                    FilePath   = [string]$matchItem.FilePath
   202→                    LineNumber = [int]$matchItem.LineNumber
   203→                    OldText    = [string]$matchItem.LineText
   204→                    NewText    = $previewNewText
   205→                })
   206→            }
   207→        }
   208→    } else {
   209→        # Replace mode: perform actual replacements, optionally creating backups
   210→        foreach ($file in $matchingFiles) {
   211→            # @() ensures foreach never receives $null when the file has no matches
   212→            foreach ($change in @(Invoke-FileReplace `
   213→                -FilePath $file `
   214→                -SearchPattern $SearchPattern `
   215→                -Replacement $Replacement `
   216→                -CreateBackup $CreateBackup)) {
   217→                $allResults.Add($change)
   218→            }
   219→        }
   220→    }
   221→
   222→    return [PSCustomObject[]]$allResults.ToArray()
   223→}
   224→
   225→# ============================================================
   226→# Function: Get-SearchReplaceSummary
   227→# Formats a human-readable summary report from change records.
   228→# Report includes: total files modified, total changes, and per-change details
   229→# grouped by file.
   230→# ============================================================
   231→function Get-SearchReplaceSummary {
   232→    [CmdletBinding()]
   233→    [OutputType([string])]
   234→    param(
   235→        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
   236→        [AllowEmptyCollection()]
   237→        [PSCustomObject[]]$Changes
   238→    )
   239→
   240→    if ($Changes.Count -eq 0) {
   241→        return [string]'No changes were made.'
   242→    }
   243→
   244→    # Aggregate statistics
   245→    [int]$totalChanges = [int]$Changes.Count
   246→    [string[]]$distinctFiles = [string[]]@($Changes | ForEach-Object { [string]$_.FilePath } | Select-Object -Unique)
   247→    [int]$totalFiles = [int]$distinctFiles.Count
   248→
   249→    [System.Text.StringBuilder]$sb = [System.Text.StringBuilder]::new()
   250→    $null = $sb.AppendLine('========================================')
   251→    $null = $sb.AppendLine('  Search and Replace Summary Report')
   252→    $null = $sb.AppendLine('========================================')
   253→    $null = $sb.AppendLine("Total files modified : $totalFiles")
   254→    $null = $sb.AppendLine("Total changes made   : $totalChanges")
   255→    $null = $sb.AppendLine('')
   256→
   257→    # Group changes by file path for a cleaner, organized report
   258→    [object[]]$grouped = @($Changes | Group-Object -Property FilePath)
   259→    foreach ($fileGroup in $grouped) {
   260→        $null = $sb.AppendLine("File: $($fileGroup.Name)")
   261→        $null = $sb.AppendLine(('-' * 60))
   262→        foreach ($change in $fileGroup.Group) {
   263→            $null = $sb.AppendLine("  Line $($change.LineNumber):")
   264→            $null = $sb.AppendLine("    OLD: $($change.OldText)")
   265→            $null = $sb.AppendLine("    NEW: $($change.NewText)")
   266→        }
   267→        $null = $sb.AppendLine('')
   268→    }
   269→
   270→    return [string]$sb.ToString()
   271→}
   272→

# SearchReplace.Tests.ps1
# TDD tests for multi-file search and replace functionality.
# Uses Pester 5 as the test framework.
# All tests use strict mode as required.

Set-StrictMode -Latest
$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'

# Import the module under test via dot-sourcing.
# $PSScriptRoot resolves to the directory containing this test file.
[string]$modulePath = Join-Path $PSScriptRoot 'SearchReplace.ps1'
. $modulePath

# ============================================================
# Helper functions for creating and removing test fixtures
# ============================================================

function New-TestFixtures {
    [CmdletBinding()]
    [OutputType([string])]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [string]$BasePath
    )

    [string]$testDir = Join-Path $BasePath 'test-fixtures'

    # Create directory structure: root + two subdirs, one with a nested subdir
    $null = New-Item -Path $testDir -ItemType Directory -Force
    $null = New-Item -Path (Join-Path $testDir 'subdir1') -ItemType Directory -Force
    $null = New-Item -Path (Join-Path $testDir 'subdir2') -ItemType Directory -Force
    $null = New-Item -Path (Join-Path $testDir 'subdir2' 'nested') -ItemType Directory -Force

    # Root-level .txt files with known content
    Set-Content -Path (Join-Path $testDir 'file1.txt') -Value @(
        'Hello World',
        'This is a test file',
        'foo bar baz',
        'The quick brown fox'
    )

    Set-Content -Path (Join-Path $testDir 'file2.txt') -Value @(
        'Another file',
        'foo appears here too',
        'Nothing special'
    )

    # .txt file in subdir1
    Set-Content -Path (Join-Path $testDir 'subdir1' 'file3.txt') -Value @(
        'Subdirectory file',
        'foo and bar'
    )

    # .log file in subdir2 (should NOT match *.txt pattern)
    Set-Content -Path (Join-Path $testDir 'subdir2' 'file4.log') -Value @(
        'Log entry: foo',
        'Log entry: bar'
    )

    # Deeply nested .txt file
    Set-Content -Path (Join-Path $testDir 'subdir2' 'nested' 'file5.txt') -Value @(
        'Deeply nested',
        'foo is here'
    )

    # .txt file with no matches for negative testing
    Set-Content -Path (Join-Path $testDir 'nomatch.txt') -Value @(
        'This file has no matches',
        'for our pattern'
    )

    return [string]$testDir
}
